All right, you're back and look what's here. Props. I have a table, a little protective coating over the top, a hammer, and a marker. Why? Well, we're going to do an exercise here about beliefs in the sense of the power of beliefs, how they form and how you get rid of them in the real world. See, what most people try to do, and it doesn't work, is they try to wish or talk away a belief. For example, you have a belief saying, I'm just not a good salesperson. I'm not a good closer. That's your belief for whatever reason. We'll explain how in a moment, right? That's your belief. So you want to now get rid of that belief and replace it with an empowering belief. Like, I'm an awesome closer. I can close anyone who's closeable. I'm a superstar salesperson. I'm a master persuader. Whatever one and very issue you like, right? So the question is, is how do you get rid of that limiting belief and replace it with an empowering belief? Because here's the deal. As you just learned, if you have that limiting belief, then you're not going to go out there and try to close or settle or persuade. You'll say, that's not who I am. It's not what I do. In fact, why should I even learn the straight line system? I'm constitutionally incapable of closing. It's me. I'm flawed. Your potential has been cut off, right? So we must. Absolutely must.
